hyprlandBoolRegistration.finding: Hyprland registers CBoolValue as Hyprlang::INT with default 1 or 0.

hyprlangIntParser.finding: Hyprlang CONFIGDATATYPE_INT uses configStringToInt; it accepts 0x hex, rgb/rgba color-style ints, prefixes true/on/yes as 1, prefixes false/off/no as 0, and numeric integers.

policyFinding.sourceBackedParserBehaviorBeyondApp: Because bool rows are integer-backed, the official parser also accepts other integer forms; this is not recommended as UI policy for boolean rows.
policyFinding.prefixCaveat: Hyprlang source uses prefix checks for true/on/yes and false/off/no. The app should not mirror prefix looseness without an explicit repair sprint.
policyFinding.recommendedFutureUiPolicy: Offer only true/false in UI controls.
policyFinding.recommendedFutureValidatorPolicy: Keep exact typed aliases or normalize exact aliases to true/false; do not broaden to arbitrary integers or prefix aliases just because Hyprland parser accepts them.

rows.recommendedPreviousAction: Prompt 3 should inspect official Hyprland bool parsing source and then choose one policy: keep aliases with normalization, restrict UI/write values to true/false, or keep parser-compatible aliases but never offer them in UI.
sourceRefs: hyprland_legacy_config_manager: CBoolValue registered as Hyprlang::INT
hyprlang_config: configStringToInt boolean word and integer parsing
